metadata.title: {product} — all channels and the whole team without add-ons
metadata.description: One inbox for sales, support, and internal requests. Connect channels and teammates without per-channel or per-agent subscription fees, then pay for stored history and files.

hero.title: All channels and the whole team without add-ons
hero.lead: {product} brings Telegram, WhatsApp, MAX, VK, Avito, website chat, email, and internal requests into one workspace. You pay for the data you store, not for every channel or agent.

marketPain.title: Pricing usually grows with connections, not with value.
marketPain.summary: When every WhatsApp, Telegram, Avito account or new teammate becomes another subscription line, companies start saving on channels and lose control of conversations.

cards.title: Channels become a budget choice
cards.description: Teams decide which touchpoints to connect even though customers already write from different messengers and marketplaces.

cards.title: Agents become a cost center
cards.description: Growing support or sales teams increases the bill even when everyone works with the same customer base.

cards.title: History stays scattered
cards.description: Conversations live in phones, personal accounts, and narrow CRM integrations that are hard to control or hand over.

pricingModel.title: Pay for stored history, files, and company data.
pricingModel.summary: Channels and teammates should not be artificial blockers. The resource that actually grows with the business is stored messages, attachments, files, and retention time.

steps.title: Connect the channels you need
steps.description: Telegram, WhatsApp, MAX, VK, Avito, website chat, email, and other providers plug in as adapters without a separate {product} channel subscription.

steps.title: Bring the whole team
steps.description: Sales, support, managers, internal teams, and shift agents work in one workspace without paying for every account.

steps.title: Control storage
steps.description: The company can see how much space text, files, attachments, and history consume, then tune retention without losing control.

pricingModel.note: External provider costs such as WABA, SMS, AI, or transcription stay separate and transparent. {product} does not turn them into hidden channel subscriptions.

channels.title: One working layer for every channel and incoming source.
channels.summary: Customers write in messengers, social networks, marketplaces, classifieds, website, and email. {product} brings everything into one workspace for sales, support, and internal teams.

stats.title: One inbox
stats.description: All incoming requests in one space. Nothing gets lost.

stats.title: Unified profile
stats.description: Conversation history from every channel in one profile. Full context stays close.

stats.title: No connection chaos
stats.description: Flexible connections and clear setup without unnecessary complexity.

channels.note: Connect the sources you need without feeling that each one is a separate tariff.

workflow.title: A platform for handling requests of any complexity.
workflow.summary: {product} brings channels, data, and processes into one system so every team works by the same rules.

items.title: Request sources
items.description: Messengers, social networks, marketplaces, classifieds, websites, email, telephony, and API.

items.title: Unified customer profile
items.description: All channels, conversations, orders, views, and files in one profile.

items.title: Processes and routing
items.description: Auto-assignment, scenarios, rules, priorities, and SLA.

items.title: Team and roles
items.description: Departments, roles, access rights, shifts, and internal comments.

items.title: Data and storage
items.description: History, attachments, events, retention, export, and action audit.

items.title: Diagnostics and reports
items.description: Channel load, efficiency, errors, and response quality.

workflow.noteLead: A channel is an entry point.
workflow.note: {product} is the system where requests are handled, stored, and controlled. You manage data, teams, and processes inside your own company.

audiences.title: For teams whose inbound requests already live in several places.
audiences.summary: When requests live across several places, teams lose context, response speed, and history control. {product} brings channels, roles, and data into one working layer for sales, support, and branches.

items.title: Sales and lead response
items.description: Leads arrive through messengers, forms, email, and chats. {product} brings them into one workspace so teams do not lose requests and respond faster.

items.title: Support and service
items.description: Requests, history, attachments, and statuses live in one window. Answer faster and resolve more.

items.title: E-commerce and marketplaces
items.description: Orders and messages from different platforms land in one feed without losing context or deadlines.

items.title: Branches and networks
items.description: Many locations, local channels, and shifts need one access and storage policy.

items.title: Enterprise and on-prem
items.description: Private environments, audit, export, and data control without forking core. {product} gives you that.

comparison.title: The market counts channels and agents. {product} counts the resource you own.

rows.label: Channels
rows.usual: Each WhatsApp, Telegram, Avito, or other account can become a separate monthly fee.
rows.hulee: Channel connection is not a separate {product} subscription line.

rows.label: Agents
rows.usual: Adding teammates often increases the subscription price.
rows.hulee: The team can grow without paying for every operator account.

rows.label: Conversations
rows.usual: Plans can cap monthly dialogues or leads.
rows.hulee: History is company data, not an artificial limit on conversations.

rows.label: Control
rows.usual: Costs become hard to predict as channels and branches grow.
rows.hulee: The bill is explained by storage, retention, AI, transcription, and external provider costs.

calculator.title: Estimate the cost before connecting.
calculator.summary: Choose channels, team size, conversation volume, and history retention. {product} shows the expected load and splits cost into clear parts: data storage, extra services, and external provider costs.

forecast.title: Your forecast
forecast.noteLead: You see a resource forecast, not channel pricing:
forecast.note: how much data must be stored and which extra services are used.

metrics.title: Channels — $0 in {product}
metrics.description: Connect any channels without a subscription fee.

metrics.title: Agents — $0 in {product}
metrics.description: The team grows, the price does not.

metrics.title: GB is the basis
metrics.description: Pay only for storage volume and services.

trust.title: Storage-based pricing only works when control is visible.
trust.summary: If storage is the paid resource, the site must show that data can be measured, retained, exported, and deployed privately.

items.title: Tenant boundary
items.description: Data, files, events, queues, API, and storage always belong to one company boundary.

items.title: Retention and export
items.description: The company chooses how long to keep history, what to archive, and how to export data.

items.title: SaaS, isolated, and on-prem
items.description: One core can run in the {product} cloud, a dedicated environment, or customer infrastructure.

plans.title: Plans differ by resources and control, not by basic channel access.
plans.summary: This keeps the main promise without pretending infrastructure is infinite: channels and teammates are not priced separately.

items.name: Start
items.description: For small teams that want to bring main channels into one inbox.

items.name: Team
items.description: For growing sales and support teams with more conversations.

items.name: Business
items.description: For companies that need control, audit, and predictable limits.

items.name: Enterprise
items.description: For isolated SaaS, on-prem, white-label, and company-specific modules.

faq.title: Questions the page should answer immediately.

items.question: Are channels really free?
items.answer: {product} does not charge a separate subscription fee just to connect a channel. If an external provider charges for WABA, SMS, AI, or transcription, that cost is shown separately.

items.question: Is there an agent limit?
items.answer: The base model does not price every operator account. Limits may exist for security, SLA, or enterprise policy reasons, but not as an artificial tax on team growth.

items.question: What counts as storage?
items.answer: Message text, files, images, audio, attachments, exportable history, and technical data the company chooses to keep.

items.question: What happens over the storage limit?
items.answer: First come warnings and retention recommendations. Hard limits should block new heavy uploads, not access to existing history and export.

cta.title: Show every channel without fearing the bill.
cta.summary: Start with one inbox, bring the team, and calculate what it really costs to store company conversation history.
